title: Closed-Lost & Win-Back: You're Losing Momentum, Not Bake-Offs
tldr: Only 1 of 25 closed-lost conference deals was a true competitive loss — most died from timing, budget/approval, or never reaching a real conversation. Win-back priority: 5 deals with live triggers (~$153k), plus Collin County ($165k).

blocks.title: You're not losing bake-offs — you're losing momentum
blocks.body: 25 closed-lost conference opps, ~$870k. Only **1** was a true competitive loss. The rest stalled on timing, budget, approval, or never reached a real conversation.

- **Only 1 of 25 was a true competitive loss.** The rest: timing, budget, approval, or never-engaged.
- **~6 deals (~$180k) died before a real conversation** — disco booked, never happened (the OK/AZ/RI cluster). The fix is booking and *holding* the first call, not a better pitch.
- **Most "losses" are "not now"** with a real re-engagement trigger: Cedarburg (Oct budget), Brazoria (after Jul 18), Llano (post-CAMA conversion), Burke (new manager incoming).
- **Biggest single prize: Collin County, $165k** — went dark after pricing; worth an exec-level re-approach.
- **Spend time first on the 5 🟢 (~$153k)** — each has a trigger you can act on now → then Collin.

blocks.heading: Where the calls disagree with the notes

blocks.body:
The useful part isn't the rep's note — it's where the call contradicts it. Only 2 of 25 have a recorded call to check, and **both disagree with the CRM**.

**Brazoria County, TX** was marked Closed Lost (3/17) with the note "emailed Marcel to set up a review call." The calls show something different: a warm contact (Carla Heath), not Marcel, just frozen for protest season until after July 18 — a timing hold, not a loss.

**Cedarburg, WI** — the note said "DC scheduled for 5/13." The call happened, and it was a 99-second budget punt, not a discovery call:

> "Probably not at this time… let's look at next year… [budget] towards October." — Mikko Hilvo, 5/13 · [call](https://us-55493.app.gong.io/call?id=6275894547300112691)

**Delta:** not "awaiting disco" — a budget deferral with a concrete October re-entry. *(Quoted from the transcript; no extracted insight yet.)*
🟢 act now, good odds · 🟡 revivable, needs work · 🔴 low odds

blocks.caption: 25 closed-lost opps across 23 accounts. Reasons marked (rep note) have no recorded call in Format yet.

cells: Timing — frozen for protest season, on hold until after July 18 {{insight:t7bcf25k1ficcvbg0l27ca73}}
Re-engage right after July 18 with the budgetary packet she asked for
Budget — punted to next FY ("look at next year… October"); from the loss call
Re-engage Aug–Sep with a ready quote for the Oct budget
Board pushback + mid-CAMA conversion; asked to pause *(rep note)*
Fresh disco with the new CM once they start
Get TJ on a call to clear the sign-off
Went dark after initial pricing (no disco) *(rep note)*
Highest $ — exec-level re-approach, find a new champion
Re-engage via the comms manager; revive the proposal
Poorly qualified ("is this a real opp") *(rep note)*
Re-qualify Kevin Fouts on budget + need
Re-book the demo — was interested enough to schedule
Re-offer a short demo on their timing
Scheduling/OOO stall *(rep note)*; only call on file is cold, different dept
Pursue the Assessor angle (Blount referral) + lock a date
Only an intro call; never advanced *(rep note)*
Can't integrate with on-prem Neumo (daily auto-indexing) *(rep note)*
Pivot to the one-time historical project (60k pages) — no integration
Lost to competitor on price; "circle back in 6 months" *(rep note)*
Timed 6-mo check-in; watch if the competitor underdelivers
Went quiet after asking to meet in May *(rep note)*
One value-led touch to Victor; low $, low effort
Too small / fit ("too small a town for automated systems") *(rep note)*
Park unless small-town pricing or a multi-dept bundle emerges

blocks.body: Most rows are rep-attested because the deal died before a recorded conversation. Once early-funnel disco calls are recorded and the loss-driver topic runs over Jan→now, the **Reason** column fills with customer quotes for the rest — the tracker format stays exactly the same.
